description_long: This object consists of a small cluster of rectangular residential buildings arranged along intersecting paved roads, resembling a compact urban neighborhood block. The buildings are primarily box-shaped with flat roofs and feature a mix of brown and white exterior surfaces, suggesting brick or concrete construction with painted or rendered upper sections. Windows are distributed in regular, symmetrical rows on the facades, and the buildings are separated by paved walkways and driveways. The layout includes a main road running horizontally and a secondary road intersecting perpendicularly, providing access to each building. The overall design is utilitarian and modern, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on functional access. The scene is likely intended to represent a small residential development or a group of townhouses in an urban setting.
description: A compact group of modern, flat-roofed residential buildings with brown and white exteriors, arranged along intersecting paved roads. The buildings feature symmetrical window rows and a functional, urban layout.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facades of the buildings, with symmetrical windows and the primary road in the foreground.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the buildings and the intersecting roads, with the main road extending to the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back sides of the buildings and the continuation of the roads, with less visible window detail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the perpendicular road and the separation between the buildings, with the main road on the right.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple main entryways with flat doors, no visible porches or decorative elements, directly accessible from paved walkways
